issue.title: Incorrect path in `dist/client/index.html`
issue.body:
### Describe the bug

This line in `dist/client/index.html` for 12.0.0-beta.1 appears to be tied to the build runner that performed the build:

```html
<script src="/@fs//home/runner/work/vite-plugin-inspect/vite-plugin-inspect/node_modules/.pnpm/@vitejs+devtools@0.1.11_@pnpm+logger@1001.0.1_typescript@5.9.3_vite@8.0.2/node_modules/@vitejs/devtools/dist/client/inject.js" type="module"></script>
```

Full file: https://app.unpkg.com/vite-plugin-inspect@12.0.0-beta.1/files/dist/client/index.html#L29

I did wonder whether this was a special path of some kind, but running the build locally it uses a different path (specific to my system), which makes me think this isn't intentional.

When I try to use `vite-plugin-inspect@12.0.0-beta.1` with a basic Vite project and open `http://localhost:5173/.vite-inspect/` I see a network error trying to load this file.

### Reproduction

https://app.unpkg.com/vite-plugin-inspect@12.0.0-beta.1/files/dist/client/index.html#L29

### System Info

```Shell
Not relevant.
```

### Used Package Manager

pnpm

### Validations

- [x] Follow our [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/antfu/.github/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)
- [x] Read the [Contributing Guide](https://github.com/antfu/contribute).
- [x] Check that there isn't already an issue that reports the same bug to avoid creating a duplicate.
- [x] Check that this is a concrete bug. For Q&A, please open a GitHub Discussion instead.
- [x] The provided reproduction is a [minimal reproducible](https://stackoverflow.com/help/minimal-reproducible-example) of the bug.

### Contributions

- [ ] I am willing to submit a PR to fix this issue
- [ ] I am willing to submit a PR with failing tests (actually just go ahead and do it, thanks!)

issue.title: fix(client): don't include `vite:devtools:injection` plugin at build time
issue.body:
<!-- DO NOT IGNORE THE TEMPLATE!

Thank you for contributing!

---

> Please be aware that vibe-coding contributions are **🚫 STRICTLY PROHIBITED**. 
> We are humans behind these open source projects, trying hard to maintain good quality and a healthy community. 
> Not only do vibe-coding contributions pollute the code, but they also drain A LOT of unnecessary energy and time from maintainers and toxify the community and collaboration.
>
> All vibe-coded, AI-generated PRs will be rejected and closed without further notice. In severe cases, your account might be banned organization-wide and reported to GitHub.
>
> **PLEASE SHOW SOME RESPECT** and do not do so.

---

Before submitting the PR, please make sure you do the following:

- Read the [Contributing Guide](https://github.com/antfu/contribute).
- Check that there isn't already a PR that solves the problem the same way to avoid creating a duplicate.
- Provide a description in this PR that addresses **what** the PR is solving and **WHY**, or reference the issue that it solves (e.g. `fixes #123`).
- Ideally, include relevant tests that fail without this PR but pass with it.

-->

- [x] <- Keep this line and put an `x` between the brackts.

### Description

When building the client, we use vite devtools plugin and this is wrong, since it will add the module at client/index.html using local dist inject path: `/@fs//home/<some-path>/.node_modules/<some-path>/@vitejs/devtools/dist/client/inject.js`.

This PR includes:
- removes `vite:devtools:injection` from `@vitejs/devtools` plugin (from vite config file at client), current `client/index.html` at npm registry contains this: `<script src="/@fs//home/runner/work/vite-plugin-inspect/vite-plugin-inspect/node_modules/.pnpm/@vitejs+devtools@0.1.11_@pnpm+logger@1001.0.1_typescript@5.9.3_vite@8.0.2/node_modules/@vitejs/devtools/dist/client/inject.js" type="module"></script>`
- adds a new Vite plugin at client to write a module we can hack later at node:  `<script src="vite-plugin-inspect-devtools-injector.js" type="module"></script>`
- adds a new middleware to replace `vite-plugin-inspect-devtools-injector.js` at client entry point: now the middleware just loads the client entry point and replaces `vite-plugin-inspect-devtools-injector.js` with the original `/@vitejs/devtools/dist/client/inject.js` but using consumer path

This PR also includes a change at playground tsconfig.json file to use `bundler` instead `node` module resolution: Vite 8 has removed node10 types.

<!-- Please insert your description here and provide especially info about the "what" this PR is solving, and "WHY" -->

### Linked Issues

fixes #169

### Additional context

<!-- e.g. is there anything you'd like reviewers to focus on? -->

comment.body: This should already be fixed on the Vite devtools side. Thanks

issue.title: `taze -w` crashes with `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'integrity')` when `packageManager` has a sha512 and the `--include` filter doesn't cover it
issue.body:
## Summary

When the root `package.json` declares `packageManager: "pnpm@<ver>+sha512.<hash>"` and `taze … -w` is run with an `--include` filter that doesn't match the package manager, taze crashes during the write phase trying to refresh the `packageManager` integrity hash. Registry data for the package manager was never fetched (it was outside the include filter), so `resolvedDep.pkgData` is undefined.

## Stack trace

```
file:///<dlx>/node_modules/taze/dist/types-BgsyRS69.mjs:1328
        const integrity = resolvedDep.pkgData.integrity?.[version];
                                              ^
TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'integrity')
    at writePackageJSON (.../taze/dist/types-BgsyRS69.mjs:1328:44)
    at writePackage (.../taze/dist/types-BgsyRS69.mjs:1537:31)
    at check (.../taze/dist/cli.mjs:3636:9)
    at async CAC.<anonymous> (.../taze/dist/cli.mjs:3916:18)
```

## Root cause

The crashing block in `writePackageJSON` (line 1316–1336 of `types-BgsyRS69.mjs`):

```js
if (key === "packageManager") {
  const value = Object.entries(dumpDependencies(pkg.resolved, "packageManager"))[0];
  if (value) {
    pkg.raw ||= {};
    const [name, versionWithCaret] = value;
    const version = versionWithCaret.replace("^", "");
    let packageManagerValue = `${name}@${version}`;
    const resolvedDep = pkg.resolved.find((dep) => dep.source === "packageManager" && dep.name === name);
    if (resolvedDep?.hexHash) {
      const integrity = resolvedDep.pkgData.integrity?.[version];   // ← here
      ...
    }
  }
}
```

`resolvedDep?.hexHash` is truthy (the original `packageManager` string contained `+sha512.…`), so taze tries to look up fresh integrity for the new version. But because the package manager wasn't matched by `--include`, taze never fetched its registry data, so `resolvedDep.pkgData` is undefined. The `?.` is on `.integrity` rather than on `.pkgData`, so the access throws.

## Fix

One-character guard:

```diff
- const integrity = resolvedDep.pkgData.integrity?.[version];
+ const integrity = resolvedDep.pkgData?.integrity?.[version];
```

If `pkgData` is missing, `integrity` ends up undefined, the surrounding `if (integrity)` block is skipped, and the existing sha512 in the `packageManager` string is preserved (which is the right behavior — we don't have new data to refresh it with).

## Reproduction

Single file. No pnpm catalog, no workspace, no install.

`package.json`:

```json
{
  "name": "taze-repro",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "private": true,
  "packageManager": "pnpm@10.29.2+sha512.bef43fa759d91fd2da4b319a5a0d13ef7a45bb985a3d7342058470f9d2051a3ba8674e629672654686ef9443ad13a82da2beb9eeb3e0221c87b8154fff9d74b8",
  "dependencies": {
    "storybook": "10.4.0-alpha.8"
  }
}
```

Then:

```
pnpm dlx taze@19.11.0 next --include storybook --include-locked -w
```

Expected: `storybook` bumped to the latest `@next` version in `package.json`.
Actual: `storybook` is bumped (in memory), then taze crashes before writing, because the `packageManager` field's integrity-refresh path hits the undefined `pkgData`.

## Variations

- Crash also fires on `taze@19.10.0`.
- Removing the sha512 from the `packageManager` string (e.g. `"pnpm@10.29.2"`) avoids the crash — `resolvedDep?.hexHash` becomes falsy and the integrity-refresh block is skipped.
- Including `pnpm` in the filter (`--include "storybook,pnpm"`) also avoids the crash. With `pnpm` in the filter, taze fetches its registry data, `pkgData` is defined, and the integrity lookup proceeds normally. The `storybook` bump is written and the `packageManager` field is preserved unchanged (since pnpm 10.29.2 is already current).

## Environment

- taze 19.11.0 (also 19.10.0)
- pnpm 10.29.2
- node 24.15.0
- Windows 11 ARM64 (cross-platform status unverified, but the bug is in package-manager metadata handling — should reproduce everywhere)

## Related

- [#196](https://github.com/antfu-collective/taze/issues/196) (closed, no fix linked) — different error message (`Cannot convert undefined or null to object`), but same shape: regression after 19.1.1, write-mode crash, undefined property access. May be the same underlying bug surfacing at a different access site.
- [#170](https://github.com/antfu-collective/taze/issues/170) (closed) — same family of write-path crashes on undefined property access.

issue.title: maturityPeriod not respected with stable-only expectation (latest bypasses filtered versions; newest includes prereleases)
issue.body:
Hi! Thanks for taze — super useful.
I’m seeing behavior that makes it hard to get “latest stable, but only if older than N days”.

### What I expect
Given:
```ts
export default defineConfig({
  mode: 'latest',
  maturityPeriod: 2,
})
```
I expect taze to avoid upgrading to versions published less than 2 days ago.

### What happens
- mode: 'newest' respects maturity filtering, but includes prereleases (-dev, -beta, -canary, etc.), which is not desired.
- mode: 'latest' is stable-only in intent, but appears to still select too-recent versions in some cases (as if maturityPeriod were bypassed).

### Repro context
Example outcomes with mode: 'newest' + maturityPeriod: 2 show prerelease targets like:
- 7.9.0-dev.4
- 16.3.0-canary.19
- 4.5.0-canary...

So newest is not suitable for stable-only workflows.

### Suspected root cause
In `src/utils/versions.ts` (`getMaxSatisfying`), `tags.latest` is used as an initial candidate for range modes, but that candidate does not appear to be validated against the already maturity-filtered versions list.

So even after maturity filtering, `tags.latest` can still be selected if it satisfies semver range.

### Suggested behavior
For the “stable + maturity” use case, one of these would help:
1. Ensure mode: 'latest' only selects `tags.latest` if it is present in the filtered version set.
2. Or add a dedicated mode for “latest stable that also satisfies maturityPeriod”.

Happy to test a fix if helpful.

issue.title: The injection script is attempting to use fileURLToPath from the client
issue.body: ### Describe the bug I'm using @vitejs/devtools@v0.1.23 and manually injecting @vitejs/devtools/client/inject on the client side, and I'm getting this error. My app is SSR-based. > DockStandalone-Y7CEWFPN-BDo6WdN9.js?v=09397d90:301 Uncaught (in promise) TypeError: (0 , import_browser_external_node_url.fileURLToPath) is not a function at DockStandalone-Y7CEWFPN-BDo6WdN9.js?v=09397d90:301:66 vite.config.ts ```js export default defineConfig({ plugins: [ vue(), nitro(), DevTools() ] }); ``` main.ts ```js if (import.meta.hot) { import('@vitejs/devtools/client/inject'); } ``` ### Reproduction Shown above ### System Info ```Shell System: OS: Linux 6.11 Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS 24.04.4 LTS (Noble Numbat) CPU: (4) x64 Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-4510U CPU @ 2.00GHz Memory: 3.03 GB / 7.68 GB Container: Yes Shell: 5.9 - /usr/bin/zsh Binaries: Node: 24.15.0 - /home/iroh/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/bin/node npm: 11.12.1 - /home/iroh/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/bin/npm bun: 1.3.14 - /home/iroh/.bun/bin/bun Browsers: Chrome: 148.0.7778.167 Firefox: 150.0.3 Firefox Developer Edition: 150.0.3 ``` ### Used Package Manager bun ### Validations - [x] Follow our [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/antfu/.github/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) - [x] Read the [Contributing Guide](https://github.com/antfu/contribute). - [x] Check that there isn't already an issue that reports the same bug to avoid creating a duplicate. - [x] Check that this is a concrete bug. For Q&A, please open a GitHub Discussion instead. - [x] The provided reproduction is a [minimal reproducible](https://stackoverflow.com/help/minimal-reproducible-example) of the bug.

issue.title: --maturity-period ignored for `latest` and `next` modes
issue.body:
## Bug

`--maturity-period N` does not filter out versions younger than N days when `mode === "latest"` (or `next`). It correctly filters for `default`, `major`, `minor`, `patch`, and `newest` modes.

## Repro

```bash
# Package with a version released < 7 days ago
taze latest --maturity-period 7 --include eslint-plugin-storybook
```

Output (today 2026-04-30, eslint-plugin-storybook 10.3.6 released 2026-04-29 = 1 day old):

```
eslint-plugin-storybook    ~2mo  ^10.2.17  →  ^10.3.6  ~1d
```

Expected: 10.3.6 filtered out, fallback to 10.3.5 (released 2026-04-07 = ~3 weeks old).

## Root cause

`getMaxSatisfying()` in `src/utils/versions.ts` (or compiled `dist/types-*.mjs:4260` in v19.11.0) returns `tags.latest` directly for `mode === "latest"` — ignores the `versions` array which is the maturity-period-filtered list.

```ts
function getMaxSatisfying(versions, current, mode, tags) {
  let version = null;
  if (mode === "latest") version = tags.latest;        // <-- skips filteredVersions
  else if (mode === "newest") version = versions.at(-1);
  else if (mode === "next") version = tags.next;       // <-- same bug
  else { /* iterates `versions` which IS the filtered list */ }
}
```

`getVersionOfRange()` correctly applies `filterVersionsByMaturityPeriod()` and passes the filtered list to `getMaxSatisfying`, but the `latest`/`next` branches discard it.

## Suggested fix

For `latest` / `next`, verify the dist-tag version is in `versions` (the filtered list). If not, fall back to `getMaxSatisfying(versions, current, "newest", tags)` or pick the highest matching version from the filtered list.

```ts
if (mode === "latest") {
  version = versions.includes(tags.latest) ? tags.latest : null
}
```

## Version

- taze: 19.11.0
- node: 25.9.0
- platform: darwin-arm64
